text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy village of Willowbrook, there lived a daring bird named Oliver. Every morning, he'd soar up onto the ancient oak at the town center. One sunny morning, Oliver decided he wanted something cooler.

He flew high and bumped against ice crystals. Then he found snow. Softer than feathers. He liked it.

While investigating deeper forests, Oliver discovered ice sheets. He slid faster than ever. How could he chill?

A mouse whispered secrets.

Oliver followed. A river slowed. It fed.

Oliver drank.

An owl watched.

That night, dreams.

Owl thought.

Perhaps chill didn.

Maybe chill ice cold owl.

He saw. Owls didn’t want cold.

Just feathers.

Oww. Ow.

Perhaps feathers soft.

I'll go quiet.

No more owls.
































Maybe chill.

Like moon light.




Dream.

Oliver slept.





































That night.

Just thoughts.

He dreamed of stars.



There
